message:
chore(beads): sync issues.jsonl with next pin-bead wave (SchemaVersion / ManifestSignature in_progress + new beads filed)

Routine drift sync absorbing the next slice of beads-tracker activity.
Captures four entries:

- flywheel_connectors-3xfmr ("SchemaVersion compatibility") moved
  open → in_progress (parallel agent picked up the work).
- flywheel_connectors-8tf1r ("ManifestSignature serde tag") moved
  open → in_progress.
- flywheel_connectors-1aerk ("MeshNodeConfig defaults") added/refreshed
  (still open).
- flywheel_connectors-6tcjg ("ConnectorPlan ordering") added/refreshed
  (still open).

Pure tracking sync — no source code, test code, or configuration
changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-19rr","title":"[IDEA-01.2] SDK runtime kit: StreamingSupervisor implementation","description":"## Goal
Implement the streaming supervisor loop with heartbeat/zombie detection and session resumption.

## Details
- Provide a reusable `StreamingSupervisor::run()` that:
  - manages connection lifecycle
  - sends heartbeats and verifies acks
  - detects zombie connections (heartbeat not acked)
  - attempts resume before full reconnect
  - emits structured health/log events
- Integrate with `FcpConnector` lifecycle (shutdown / backpressure).

## Tests
- Deterministic unit tests for zombie detection and resume fallback.
- Integration test using an in-process fake websocket stream.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A reference streaming connector can swap its loop for `StreamingSupervisor` without behavioral regressions.
- Health transitions are visible and deterministic.","notes":"Added resume fallback test + reconnect_count assertion; heartbeat timeout/log test already covers zombie detection. Implementation/testing complete; waiting on dependency flywheel_connectors-1n78.20.1 to close.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1ah","title":"fcp.postgresql: PostgreSQL Database Connector","description":"# fcp.postgresql - PostgreSQL Database Connector


## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant PostgreSQL connector for safe SQL query/transaction execution and optional LISTEN/NOTIFY streaming.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.
- For DB connectors, constrain raw TCP connects to explicit host:port (or unix socket path) only; deny private ranges unless explicitly allowed by policy.

## Overview
PostgreSQL database connector for SQL queries, transactions, and connection pooling.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Database access (SQL) + (optional) LISTEN/NOTIFY streaming

## Key Operations

### Query Operations
- `pg.query` - Execute parameterized SQL query
- `pg.execute` - Execute non-returning statement
- `pg.batch` - Execute multiple statements
- `pg.prepared` - Execute prepared statement
- `pg.explain` - Explain query plan

### Transaction Operations
- `pg.transaction.begin` - Start transaction
- `pg.transaction.commit` - Commit transaction
- `pg.transaction.rollback` - Rollback transaction
- `pg.transaction.savepoint` - Create savepoint
- Isolation levels: READ COMMITTED, REPEATABLE READ, SERIALIZABLE

### Schema Operations
- `pg.schema.tables` - List tables
- `pg.schema.columns` - Get column info
- `pg.schema.indexes` - List indexes
- `pg.schema.constraints` - Get constraints

### Advanced Features
- COPY protocol for bulk load/unload
- LISTEN/NOTIFY for event handling
- Row streaming for large results
- JSON/JSONB operations
- Full-text search support
- PostGIS spatial queries

### Connection Pool
- Configurable pool size
- Connection health checks
- Statement timeout
- Query logging

## Authentication
- Password authentication
- SSL/TLS connections
- Certificate authentication
- AWS IAM (RDS)
- Cloud SQL IAM (GCP)

## Providers Supported
- PostgreSQL (self-hosted)
- AWS RDS/Aurora
- Google Cloud SQL
- Azure Database
- Supabase
- Neon
- CockroachDB (compatible)

## Zone Architecture

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

\`\`\`
Zone 0 (Public):     Schema metadata
Zone 1 (Authorized): Read queries
Zone 2 (Private):    Write operations
Zone 3 (Sensitive):  DDL, admin operations
\`\`\`

## Security
- Parameterized queries (no SQL injection)
- Query whitelisting option
- Statement timeout enforcement
- Connection limit enforcement

## Dependencies
- fcp-core, fcp-config, fcp-testkit, fcp-telemetry

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1d8q","title":"[IDEA-13] Manifest Capability-ID Lint (no hostnames/ports)","description":"# Manifest Capability-ID Lint (no hostnames/ports)

## Goal
Add a mechanical lint that rejects capability IDs containing hostnames/ports, enforcing the FCP2 rule that network constraints belong in `network_constraints`.

## Background / Why
The compliance checklist explicitly forbids hostnames/ports in capability IDs, but enforcement is manual today. A lint prevents subtle policy bypasses.

## Scope
- Lint in manifest validation and/or CLI check.
- Clear error message with guidance.

## Deliverables
- Lint rule in `fcp-manifest`.
- Optional CLI warning in `fcp new --check` and `fcp manifest fix`.

## Tests
- Unit tests with bad capability IDs (contains `.com`, `:443`, etc.).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invalid capability IDs fail validation with actionable messages.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3tja","title":"Mesh Trace Capture + Replay Debugger","description":"# Mesh Trace Capture + Replay Debugger

## Goal
Enable deterministic capture and replay of mesh traffic for debugging and incident analysis.

## Why
- Reproduce complex mesh failures offline.
- Provide evidence bundles for audits and regressions.
- Improve developer velocity for hard-to-debug issues.

## Scope
- Trace format with redaction policy.
- Capture hooks in MeshNode (routing/admission/leases).
- Replay engine + CLI with deterministic playback.

## Non-goals
- Full production tracing UI; CLI only for now.

## Deliverables
- Trace schema + canonical encoding rules.
- Redaction policy (PII, secrets, tokens).
- MeshNode capture with sampling and size caps.
- Replay engine + `fcp trace replay` CLI.
- Unit/integration tests + E2E script with structured logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Captures can be replayed to reproduce routing/admission decisions.
- Sensitive fields are redacted or encrypted.
- Deterministic outputs for identical traces.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3z2","title":"fcp.spotify: Library Management","description":"# fcp.spotify: Library Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable safe access to the user’s library:
- list saved tracks/albums/shows
- save/unsave items

Library writes modify user state and must be auditable.

## Scope
- Get saved tracks/albums/shows/episodes (pagination).
- Save/unsave items.
- Check whether items are saved.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.library.read`
- `spotify.library.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For writes:
  - receipts + audit events include item ids and action
  - never log track names or personal context

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.library.tracks.list`
  - `spotify.library.tracks.save`
  - `spotify.library.tracks.remove`
  - `spotify.library.albums.list`
  - `spotify.library.albums.save/remove`
  - `spotify.library.shows.list/save/remove`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - save/unsave idempotency
  - audit/receipt for writes

## Acceptance criteria
- Library operations are reliable, safe, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3zhv","title":"[IDEA-01.4] SDK runtime kit: tests + reference examples","description":"## Goal
Provide reference examples and comprehensive tests for the runtime kit.

## Details
- Add a minimal streaming example (fake gateway) and polling example (fake API) in testkit.
- Add golden-log snapshots for health transitions (dependent on flywheel_connectors-11g2 schema).
- Document usage patterns in `docs/STUDY_Clawdbot_Connector_Patterns.md` and/or SDK README.

## Tests
- Unit + integration tests for both supervisors.
- Validate logging against JSONL schema (once available).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Examples compile and can be used as copy/paste templates by connector authors.
- Tests cover common failure modes (timeouts, 429, disconnect, resume).","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-41uql","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: MeshSessionHello transcript sign/verify field-binding fuzz","description":"fcp_protocol::session::MeshSessionHello::sign + verify (session.rs:374-
390) is the Ed25519 transcript signature that proves a hello is
genuine. The transcript is:
  b"FCP2-HELLO-V1" || from || to || eph_pubkey || nonce || cookie ||
  timestamp || suites || transport_limits
(canonical-CBOR encoded per field, see transcript_bytes session.rs:356-
367).

This is the hello's authentication binding — distinct from:
  - verify_cookie / compute_cookie (674lv): the stateless HMAC gate on
    a cookie key (DoS-prevention layer)
  - verify_session_mac (session_metamorphic): the per-frame MAC over
    derived session keys
  - NodeKeyAttestation::verify (xedeu): owner-issued attestation over
    node identity

A regression in the hello transcript would let an attacker forge or
pivot a captured hello — the responder accepts under a different
(from, to, eph_pubkey, ...) tuple than was signed. None of the existing
fuzz targets exercise the per-field binding of the hello transcript.

Properties asserted:

  1. **Round-trip**: sign(key, h) → verify(key.pub, h) returns Ok.
  2. **Per-field binding (×7)**: bit-flipping any one of
     (from, to, eph_pubkey, nonce, cookie, timestamp, suites,
     transport_limits) MUST cause verify to return InvalidSignature.
  3. **Wrong-key rejection**: a hello signed under key A MUST NOT
     verify under any different key B.
  4. **MissingSignature on unsigned**: a hello with signature=None
     MUST yield SessionError::MissingSignature on verify.

  Once-gated regression anchors:
    (a) Domain-tag binding via timestamp: a hello signed at t=1 MUST
        NOT verify at t=2 (timestamp dropped from transcript would
        let captured hellos replay forever — same regression class
        as the verify_cookie 674lv anchor, but for the inner
        transcript path).
    (b) eph_pubkey binding: hello signed with eph_pubkey A MUST NOT
        verify with eph_pubkey B (mid-handshake ephemeral swap-in
        surface, but at the inner-transcript layer).

Acceptance:
  - fuzz/fuzz_targets/hello_transcript_binding.rs
  - 4 properties + 2 once-gated anchors above.
  - Registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml.
  - cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:26:46.117404Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T22:28:21.487857Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T22:28:21.487392Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3095,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-41uql","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit ff327edd. fuzz/fuzz_targets/hello_transcript_binding.rs — 4 properties (round-trip, per-field binding ×7 with no-op-mutation guard, wrong-key rejection, MissingSignature on unsigned) + 2 once-gated anchors for timestamp binding (parallel to 674lv cookie anchor) and eph_pubkey binding at the transcript layer. Distinct from verify_cookie/verify_session_mac/NodeKeyAttestation surfaces. cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:28:21Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-6r8","title":"fcp.arxiv: Reference extraction from paper content","description":"# fcp.arxiv: Reference extraction from paper content (FCP2)

## Goal
Extract structured references from paper PDFs/LaTeX to support:
- citation linking
- bibliography reconstruction
- cross-referencing with external indices

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Scope
- Parse references section from extracted text.
- Produce a normalized `ReferenceList`:
  - title (best-effort)
  - authors (best-effort)
  - venue/year (best-effort)
  - external identifiers when detectable (DOI/arXiv id)

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `arxiv.references.extract`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- No network beyond pinned PDF hosts.
- Bounded processing:
  - cap pages/bytes parsed
  - cap number of extracted references

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `arxiv.paper.references.extract`
- Canonical output type:
  - `ReferenceList`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Fixture-based tests with known-paper PDFs.
- Golden extraction vectors for known reference formats.

## Acceptance criteria
- Reference extraction is deterministic and bounded.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","title":"[FCP3/P4.4] Migrate a first connector family end to end without compatibility wrappers","description":"Background: architectural contracts are not credible until at least one real connector family uses them end to end. A narrow adaptation provides a proving ground before broad migration across all connector archetypes.

Scope: pick a first connector family and migrate it through the new host and SDK execution model without adding compatibility wrappers that preserve the old architecture. The chosen family should exercise the important lifecycle and evidence concepts while remaining narrow enough to finish. Produce deterministic fixtures, crate local unit tests, integration tests, end to end test scripts, migration transcripts, and detailed debug logs that later integration, pilot, and documentation beads can reuse rather than rebuilding ad hoc examples.

Acceptance: one connector family runs end to end on the new contracts, and the migration leaves a repeatable pattern plus reproducible tests, logs, and artifacts for additional families rather than a one off exception.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.631687Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:53:31.109081Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:53:31.108425Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connector-migration","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-4","pilot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:35.473041Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:34.870529Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:34.260886Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2225,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose claiming P4.4. Plan: migrate the cron connector family as the pilot — it's the simplest local-first connector (no external auth, in-memory state, 5 operations). Will exercise the new host/SDK execution model end-to-end and produce a repeatable migration pattern.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:19:33Z"},{"id":2233,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose completed the cron connector pilot migration (commit 46dc8958). CronConnector now implements FcpConnector trait with all required methods. 215 tests pass (169 lib + 46 integration). Pattern established: trait impl coexists with handle_* methods for mutable operations. No compatibility wrappers.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:32:17Z"},{"id":2234,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Messaging connector family (Telegram + Discord) verified as E2E migration proof.

TELEGRAM (306 tests: 250 lib + 2 bin + 54 integration):
- fcp_async_core runtime (Builder::new_multi_thread, task::JoinHandle, broadcast/watch channels) ✓
- ConnectorRuntime + RetryLoop from fcp-sdk ✓
- ConnectorErrorMapping trait fully implemented ✓
- PollingCursor + PollingSupervisor for singleton-writer lease ✓
- CapabilityVerifier for default-deny enforcement ✓
- manifest.toml with 5 operations, 5 events, rate limits, capabilities ✓
- No compatibility wrappers, no legacy imports ✓

DISCORD (524 tests: 235 lib + 226 integration + 63 extra):
- Same contract stack: ConnectorRuntime + RetryLoop + ConnectorErrorMapping ✓
- Same JSON-RPC protocol loop pattern ✓
- No compatibility wrappers ✓

REPEATABLE PATTERN:
1. main.rs: fcp_async_core::runtime::Builder → JSON-RPC stdin/stdout loop → dispatch to handle_* methods
2. client.rs: ConnectorRuntime + RetryLoop + AttemptOutcome for retry decisions
3. error.rs: ConnectorErrorMapping trait (from_async_error, to_fcp_error, is_retryable, retry_after)
4. connector.rs: handle_configure/handshake/health/doctor/self_check/introspect/simulate/invoke/shutdown
5. manifest.toml: operations + events + rate_limits + capabilities + zones + sandbox
6. tests/integration.rs: wiremock-based lifecycle + operation + error taxonomy coverage

Both connectors run on new kernel/SDK contracts with zero compat wrappers. Pattern is documented and reproducible for additional families.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-6ui4t","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-7: DecodeStatus transcript binding + oversized-hint anti-DoS guard","description":"DecodeStatus (fcp-protocol/src/fcps.rs) is the per-progress status the receiver sends during a transfer. Its transcript binds to (object_id, zone_id, zone_key_id, epoch_id, recipient_node_id, request_nonce, received_unique, needed, complete, missing_hint). Zero conformance coverage today. Critical: verify() runs validate_hint_bounds BEFORE building the transcript so an attacker cannot force a multi-MB transcript materialization on every verify call (NORMATIVE anti-amplification rule). Add decode_status_signing_conformance.rs pinning round-trip, wrong-key, per-field transcript binding, oversized-hint pre-verify rejection, and the documented header-is-context-only separation. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-protocol).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:47:46.572230Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:49:06.922212Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:49:06.921731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","protocol","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3010,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6ui4t","author":"jemanuel","text":"2039cc53 adds decode_status_signing_conformance.rs with 15 tests pinning DecodeStatus FCP2-DECODE-STATUS-V2 transcript: round-trip + wrong-key, per-field binding (object_id/zone_id/zone_key_id/epoch_id/recipient/request_nonce/received_unique/needed/complete-flag/missing_hint contents+presence), oversized-hint pre-transcript rejection (anti-amplification guard at MAX_MISSING_HINT_ENTRIES+1), and header-is-context-only separation. All 15 pass in <0.01s. Uses only public fcp_protocol API.","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:49:06Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8t034","title":"[REVIEW-CONVERGE][MEDIUM] fcp-bootstrap certificate chain accepts expired certs (no validity period check)","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1240-1270 (commit 1889e2df br-jx3r4) certificate_has_x509_issuer_chain verifies ONLY the cryptographic signature via x509_parser verify_signature. It does NOT check the validity period (not_before / not_after) or any revocation signal (CRL/OCSP). A provisioning certificate whose not_after has passed still chains cleanly and select_certificate_for_provisioning returns it as the winning candidate. For a bootstrap flow that mints owner identity, this means a long-decommissioned identity can still drive genesis creation until the token is physically retired. Suggested fix: after verify_signature, also check that tbs.validity.not_before <= now and tbs.validity.not_after > now (a clock injection seam already exists elsewhere in fcp-bootstrap for deterministic tests). Also consider leaf-only EKU check for code-signing / key-cert-sign depending on what the provisioning certificate is used for. Reviewer: cc_1 REVIEW-CONVERGE.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:08:48.505654Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:21:00.616003Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:21:00.615690Z","close_reason":"Rejected expired and not-yet-valid X.509 provisioning chains during certificate selection","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8tv","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Zone architecture for prod safety (dev/staging/prod isolation)","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Zone architecture for prod safety (dev/staging/prod isolation) (FCP2)

## Goal
Define a safe-by-default zone strategy for Kubernetes operations, especially production.

Important: the connector instance is **single-zone bound**. “Dev/staging/prod isolation” is achieved by deploying separate connector instances bound to separate zones and pinned to separate cluster API hosts.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Scope
- Recommended zone naming patterns (policy-owned):
  - `z:work:k8s:dev:<cluster>`
  - `z:work:k8s:staging:<cluster>`
  - `z:work:k8s:prod:<cluster>`
- Capability tiering guidance:
  - readonly vs deploy vs exec
  - forbid deletes in prod by default
- Approval guidance:
  - prod deploy and exec should require ApprovalToken

## Deliverables
- Documented default policy expectations (reason codes + deny-by-default rules) referenced by connector operations.
- Clear mapping between zone policy and Kubernetes RBAC contexts.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Policy-oriented tests (mock-only) verifying:
  - prod zone denies mutations by default
  - approval gating required for exec/deploy

## Acceptance criteria
- A user can set up three connector instances and be mechanically confident prod is protected.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8v34","title":"TEST: Enrollment Unit Tests (DeviceEnrollment, NodeKeyAttestation, Removal Workflow)","description":"# TEST: Enrollment Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that device enrollment and removal work correctly and securely.

## Test Categories

### DeviceEnrollment Object
- [ ] Valid enrollment parses correctly
- [ ] Owner signature verifies
- [ ] Tailscale node_id binding correct
- [ ] Authorized tags match
- [ ] Expiry (if present) enforced
- [ ] Malformed enrollment rejected

### NodeKeyAttestation
- [ ] Attestation binds node_id to pubkeys
- [ ] signing_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] encryption_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] issuance_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] KID mappings correct
- [ ] Owner signature verifies
- [ ] Attestation expiry enforced

### Enrollment Flow
- [ ] New node can enroll with valid enrollment object
- [ ] Enrolled node receives zone keys (ZoneKeyManifest)
- [ ] Enrolled node can participate in sessions
- [ ] Enrolled node appears in peer discovery

### Key Rotation
- [ ] Node can rotate its keys
- [ ] New attestation replaces old
- [ ] Old KIDs become invalid
- [ ] Sessions using old keys gracefully terminated

### Device Removal
- [ ] Removal revokes enrollment object
- [ ] Removal revokes attestation
- [ ] Removal revokes issuer keys
- [ ] Tokens from removed node rejected
- [ ] Zone keys rotated after removal
- [ ] Removed node cannot participate

### Attack Scenarios (Adversarial)
- [ ] Replay old enrollment: rejected (revoked or expired)
- [ ] Forge enrollment: invalid owner signature rejected
- [ ] Use removed node keys: rejected after revocation propagates
- [ ] Impersonate node_id: attestation signature check fails

## Golden Vectors
- Valid DeviceEnrollment bytes
- Valid NodeKeyAttestation bytes
- Removal workflow sequence

## Integration Tests
- [ ] Full enrollment → operation → removal cycle
- [ ] Multi-node enrollment
- [ ] Concurrent enrollment attempts

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "node_id": "...",
  "operation": "enroll|attest|rotate|remove",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Enrollment flow works end-to-end
- Removal flow invalidates all node authority
- No stale credentials accepted after removal
